checks.a_analysis: The output content was compared page-by-page with the source PDF. All text elements in the JSON, including titles, headings, and body text, correspond directly to information present in the source slides. No fabricated sections, introductions, or conclusions were identified. Captions and summaries for tables are present but are permitted as AI-generated metadata for accessibility.

Every contact detail was verified. The email addresses for Julia Lerche, Emma Sandoe, and Amanda Van Vleet on page 2 are correct. The general contact email 'Medicaid.NCEngagement@dhhs.nc.gov' appearing on pages 9 and 10 matches the source. The mailing address for the NC Medicaid Section 1115 Waiver Team (1950 Mail Service Center, Raleigh NC 27699-1950) on page 9 is accurate. The URL 'medicaid.ncdhhs.gov/transformation/proposed-program-design' on page 9 also matches the source exactly.
All 10 pages of the source PDF are accounted for in the JSON structure. Each slide's content, from the cover page to the final Q&A slide, is represented in the 'sections' array.

The document contains two main informational structures converted to tables. The contact information table in section 2 correctly includes all three individuals and their respective titles and emails. The timeline in section 4, while visually a graphic in the PDF, is accurately captured as a table containing all six milestone dates and descriptions. No data rows were lost.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text within each section was cross-referenced. For example, on page 5, the specific criteria for mandatory enrollment (ICF-IID, Transitions to Community Living, Innovations/TBI 1915(c) waiver, state-funded residential treatment) are all preserved. Similarly, the detailed proposed policy changes for dual eligibles on page 6 and the pilot-related modifications on page 7 are fully captured without significant omission.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Minor differences in whitespace or punctuation are consistent with acceptable reformatting for JSON. No wording changes were found that alter the legal or programmatic meaning of the document. Numerical values (e.g., '$86.6 million', 'June 30, 2026', '20 months') all match the source exactly.

checks.a_analysis: The output document does not contain any duplicated content. While the hearing title appears in multiple sections (e.g., Section 1 and Section 2), this is faithful to the source document where the title is prominently displayed on both the title slide (Page 1) and the presenter slide (Page 2).

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. While the document starts at level H2 rather than H1, there are no skips in the hierarchy (e.g., H2 to H4). Section titles are appropriately mapped to elements, and sub-sections within slides use H3. This is acceptable normalization for accessibility.

The tables in the output (Section 2 for presenters and Section 4 for the timeline) are structurally sound, maintaining the correct number of rows and columns as interpreted from the visual grid of the source. Headers are correctly identified in the presenter table.

The reading order for sequential data is significantly broken in two places. First, the timeline on page 4 is converted to a 2-row table that groups points by their vertical position (top row/bottom row) rather than their chronological sequence. Reading row-by-row results in a non-sequential flow (e.g., jumping from Summer 2023 back to July 2021). Second, the presenter contact info on page 2 is converted to a table that reads all names, then all titles, then all emails, rather than reading each person's complete info block as intended by the source's logical flow.
checks.b_evidence_source: Visual timeline sequence: Nov 2019 -> July 2021 -> Early 2022 -> Dec 2022 -> Summer 2023 -> Oct 2024.
checks.b_evidence_output: Table rows in Section 4: Row 1 contains [November 2019, Early 2022, Summer 2023]; Row 2 contains [July 2021, December 2022, October 2024].

checks.a_analysis: In Section 9 (Comment Process), the list structure is broken. The mailing address, which is visually and logically part of the third list item in the source, is rendered as a standalone paragraph element outside the list in the output. This splits the relevant information and breaks the structural relationship defined in the original document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Bullet point 3: "Written comments may be mailed to the following address; please indicate “NC Section 1115 Waiver” in the written message:" followed by the indented address.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list ends at index 45; the address is a separate paragraph at index 46.

checks.a_analysis: Hyperlinks and email addresses are inconsistently handled. While some emails in Section 2 are correctly converted to 'mailto' links, several prominent URLs and email addresses (e.g., the URL on Page 9 and the email on Page 10) are rendered as simple text objects without 'href' or 'url' properties, rendering them unclickable.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text block for the URL in Section 9 and the email in Section 10 are text objects without an 'href' property.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully translates the visual organization of the source PDF's slides into a coherent single-page document. Multi-column elements, such as the contact information on page 2 and the timeline on page 4, have been appropriately restructured into tables or linear flows that preserve the original's intent and logic. Sidebar content (e.g., the Tribal Uncompensated Care Pool box on slide 3) is integrated into the primary flow without loss of context.

The tables used for the 'Presenters Contact Information' (derived from slide 2) and the 'Demonstration Timeline' (derived from slide 4) are correctly rendered. Columns and rows are well-aligned, headers are distinct, and the data remains perfectly readable and understandable. The conversion of the staggered timeline from the PDF into a grid-based table is an effective visual mapping.

The only meaningful image in the source document is the Great Seal of the State of North Carolina on the title page, which is present and correctly rendered in the HTML output. Decorative icons and graphical layout elements (like the timeline arrows) have been appropriately converted to text/tables or omitted per accessibility guidelines.

There is no visual duplication of body content. While titles and headings appear in the Table of Contents navigation area, their appearance in the main body is correct and intended for document structure. No paragraphs, tables, or sections are repeated within the body content itself.
